title: Parallels with 2001 A Space Odyssey
wikitext:
[]: Parallels with "2001: A Space Odyssey"?             Quote Post by Mr JeburtO » Tue Mar 25, 2008 3:09 pm Im probably reading into this to much, and i am writing my dissertation right now so this may be a brief argument, but i'll hopefully flesh it out tonight if i get all this typing finished anywho....heres my argument. in 2001 the simplified story is: 1) Being found on earth (the monolith) which instigates the evolution of mankind from apes. 2) 2nd being (another monolith) uncovered on the moon which sends a transmission to Jupiter (or Saturn in the book). 3)Mankind travels to Jupiter where another monolith is discovered floating in space, which, when united with man instigates another forced evolution, evolving man into a higher life form, a being of pure energy who overseas the evolution of a new species on Europa (a moon of Jupiter) So what are the parallels you ask? Well... 1) The first being which instigates the evolution of man is Lilith (supposedly), 2) The 2nd being on the moon is what we see at the end of rebuild, 3) We all know the ultimate aim of Seele is to inistigate the human instrumentality project and evolve humanity into a singular entity. 4) The final being who looks over the evolution of a new race would be Shinji, (although he rejects instrumentality he becomes the "caretaker" of earth if u will) **ive noticed i used instigate a lot =/** So similar on purpose or pure coincidence? you decide Not much of an argument i know but i don't have time and it just poped into my head, i had to write it before i forgot. Plus when 2.0 comes out my theory my be kicked in the balls, but its fun to over analyse :) oh and this looks familiar tho the uncovering of the "moon giant" compared with back to the essay woo and i hope when i return people tell me how ridiculous this is (bit easier on the eyes now)

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Mar 25, 2008 3:19 pm Bloody Hell I never thought about this before, but you're right. We know Monoliths themselves are rather obvious reference to 2001 and it is unclear if certain sun-earth-moon scenery is reference to 2001 or Space Runaway Ideon. That goes either: Space Odyssey --> EoE + Ep 12 or Space Odyssey --> Space Runaway Ideon: Be Invoked --> EoE + Ep 12

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 25, 2008 3:21 pm Off-topic sourpussery . Mmkay, carry on.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr JeburtO » Tue Mar 25, 2008 3:29 pm Reichu wrote: Off-topic sourpussery . Mmkay, carry on.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Tue Mar 25, 2008 6:36 pm I think you've got something here, Mr JeburtO.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr JeburtO » Tue Mar 25, 2008 8:00 pm Decided to flesh out the importance of the "moon giant" so bare with me. In 2001 the first monolith, after stimulating the the evolution of man, vanishes. So in theory the monolith on the moon may be the same one that appeared on earth, waiting to be discovered in order to send a message to its superiors , signaling that humanity is ready for the final phase of the human evolutionary process. So relating this to Evangelion instead of the first monolith representing Lilith the 'mother' of humanity it instead represents Adam. The discovery of Adam initiated second impact, allowing man to create the basis for a new 'God', prepairing the next phase in the human evolutionary process. After second impact what remained of Adam landed on the moon waiting until a time where it would be discovered by man (in this case Seele), signaling to the Angels that the time was right to initiate the next step in human evolution. (Thats why the "moon giant" looks so shriveled as it as just been discovered and why the angels decided to appear 15 years after second impact) As Lilith had already been discovered, but 'silenced' by using the Lance of Longinus, she would be unable to send the signal to the Angels upon her connection with man. Take that conspiracy theorists :p

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Mar 25, 2008 8:22 pm http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=4161 This should be of some relevance. It is particularly of note that, according to the Proposal, the Angels were initially ARTIFACTS LEFT BY THE FAR.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Wed Mar 26, 2008 10:58 am I was going to post the same caps when i first saw it, but i thought i'd get jumped for it. funnily enough, furu and i were talking about FAR being the monoliths and this thread was kinda in the same vein. also, your avatar needs MOAR yoko.

[]:             Quote Post by eva unit 04 » Wed Mar 26, 2008 2:21 pm Very interesting,especially the part about the evolution of man into beings of pure energy in comparisson to the new "adams"creatures.Another thing i thought of was the whole "caretaker of earth"thing,which got me to thinking about the screenshot of gg with the crosses reflected in her glasses.So what if that screen cap of gg is taking place on earth during third impact in whcih the earth was covered in similar crosses.Just a theory, great find though!

[]:             Quote Post by RandomStuff » Wed Mar 26, 2008 8:20 pm Mr JeburtO wrote: In 2001 the first monolith, after stimulating the the evolution of man, vanishes. So in theory the monolith on the moon may be the same one that appeared on earth, waiting to be discovered in order to send a message to its superiors , signaling that humanity is ready for the final phase of the human evolutionary process.

[]:             Quote Post by royly » Wed Mar 26, 2008 9:41 pm great idea! But did it mean the director has out of ideas?Nomatter how 2001: A Space Odyssey and EVA are both great works! I'm reading the novel of 2001: A Space Odyssey

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Mar 27, 2008 11:47 am RandomStuff wrote: Mr JeburtO wrote: In 2001 the first monolith, after stimulating the the evolution of man, vanishes. So in theory the monolith on the moon may be the same one that appeared on earth, waiting to be discovered in order to send a message to its superiors , signaling that humanity is ready for the final phase of the human evolutionary process.

[]:             Quote Post by JWayc » Thu Mar 27, 2008 1:26 pm I was thinking the exact same thing. I might be biased, though, because 2001 and Evangelion are my two favorite films.

[]:             Quote Post by Victorthebattousai » Thu Mar 27, 2008 11:53 pm An interesting theory, anyway. But, it doesn't seem like a very EVA thing to go and follow along with 2001 so much, Usually EVA's pretty original in how it goes. But, it's certainly a possibility.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Mar 28, 2008 6:51 am Victorthebattousai wrote: But, it doesn't seem like a very EVA thing to go and follow along with 2001 so much, Usually EVA's pretty original in how it goes.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Fri Mar 28, 2008 7:06 am UrsusArctos wrote: Victorthebattousai wrote: But, it doesn't seem like a very EVA thing to go and follow along with 2001 so much, Usually EVA's pretty original in how it goes.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Fri Mar 28, 2008 7:29 am UrsusArctos wrote: Eva isn't as original as most people think it is.

[]:             Quote Post by CtHuLhU » Mon Mar 31, 2008 8:49 am Right "parallels" must be taken from dramas " Lorelei " and " Japan sinks ", like authors speak.
